title: Makefile: refactor ginkgo * ginkgo-remote
body:
Create a new common target, ginkgo-run, with the main ginkgo
test incantation; ginkgo and ginkgo-remote now invoke that,
with a clearer distinction between their arguments.

Reason for this: 'make remoteintegration' was not generating
ginkgo logs in CI. Reason: '-debug' option was missing from
the ginkgo incantation. Reason: impossibility of maintaining
duplicate long-complicated lines. This PR distills the common
aspects, making the differences clearer between local & remote.

IMPORTANT NOTE: '-nodes 3' was also missing from remote ginkgo.
This PR adds it. If this was intentionally omitted, please let
me know (with reasons) so I can refactor that too.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> Reason: '-debug' option was missing from the ginkgo incantation. 

Actually I'm pretty sure it's also the `-nodes 3` change as well.  Without that, the ginkgo output would be 100% the same as a single-node log file.  So ***caution*** is needed, I think we setup the remote tests to run single-threaded for a reason:  It will make debugging the "server" side of remote testing _MUCH_ harder (b/c centralized podman logging?).

Anyway...let's see what the actual CI jobs say about this change...

Timing results, local vs remote:

| int | distro | root | host | local | remote |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | ---: | ---: |
| int | fedora-32 | root | host | 30:50 | 46:29 |
| int | fedora-33 | root | host | 30:33 | 40:28 |
| int | ubuntu-2004 | root | host | 38:23 | 01:10:30 |
| int | ubuntu-2010 | root | host | 36:10 | 51:29 |

The `-nodes 3` doesn't seem to have made any difference in remote.
